versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 34 This is the word of the LORD that came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning Elam at the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah king of Judah. 35 This is what the LORD of Hosts says:
“Behold, I will shatter Elam’s bow,
the mainstay of their might.
36 I will bring the four winds against Elam
from the four corners of the heavens,
and I will scatter them
to all these winds.
There will not be a nation
to which Elam’s exiles will not go.
37 So I will shatter Elam before their foes,
before those who seek their lives.
I will bring disaster upon them,
even My fierce anger,”
declares the LORD.
“I will send out the sword after them
until I finish them off.
38 I will set My throne in Elam,
and destroy its king and officials,”
declares the LORD.
39 “Yet in the last days,
I will restore Elam from captivity,”
declares the LORD.

context_lines: In **Jeremiah 49**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Jeremiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **Judgment on Elam** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Jeremiah 49** remains the first control on meaning.
